brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +56.7% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$111.25 (Apr 6 AM); Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz; 45-day ceasefire rejected; OPEC+ +206K bpd for May; JPMorgan warns $150+ if disruptions continue
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $109.03/bbl. Apr 6 pre-market: ~$111.25/bbl. Markets closed Apr 3-5 (Good Friday/Easter weekend). Peak $127.61 during conflict. March +~55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. Trump sets Tuesday Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline — threatens strikes on power plants and bridges. 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egyptian-Pakistani-Turkish mediators rejected by Iran. OPEC+ agreed Apr 5 to increase output 206K bpd for May — analysts dismiss as 'academic.' JPMorgan warns oil could spike above $150/bbl if disruptions continue into mid-May. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / JPMorgan / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +68.1% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$111.81 (Apr 6); WTI-Brent spread remains inverted; gas $4.119 nationally; Apr 7 8 PM ET Hormuz deadline; dual chokepoint risk; $200/bbl scenarios modeled
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 settle: $111.54/bbl. Apr 6: ~$111.81/bbl. WTI-Brent spread remains inverted — WTI is 'the primary vehicle for traders betting on duration of US involvement' (Rigzone). Gas at $4.119/gal nationally (Apr 6 AAA). Diesel ~$5.43/gal. California $5.929/gal. Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline — Trump threatens power plants and bridges. Some US officials and Wall Street analysts have modeled $200/bbl scenarios. IEA: 'April will be much worse than March' as pre-war cargoes exhausted. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / JPMorgan / IEA / Rigzone.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.139 / +38.2% since Feb 27 — $4.119 nationally (Apr 6 AAA) — CA $5.929 — HI $5.595 — WA $5.386 — OK $3.272 (lowest) — diesel ~$5.43 — surged $1+ in one month
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 6: $4.119/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.139 surge in 38 days. Crossed $4 for first time since August 2022. California $5.929/gal (highest). Hawaii $5.595. Washington $5.386. Oklahoma $3.272 (lowest). Diesel ~$5.43/gal nationally, CA diesel $7.685. Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. Monthly jump exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -296 pts / -4.3% — S&P 6,582.69 (Apr 2 close); futures ~6,631-6,647 (Apr 6 pre-market); markets reopen Mon after Easter; VIX pre-market 24.48-24.88; Apr 7 deadline looms; Moody's recession 49%
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: 6,582.69. Markets closed Apr 3-5 (Good Friday/Easter weekend). S&P 500 futures pre-market Apr 6: ~6,631-6,647. First weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4% week ending Apr 2). VIX: 23.87 close, pre-market 24.48-24.88. Index remains ~1.54% YTD negative and ~4% from Jan 27 ATH. VIX peaked at 60.13 during early March panic, now retreating. Moody's recession probability 49%. Gold ~$4,665-4,677/oz. 10Y 4.31%. Trump Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline — binary risk event for Monday session. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / BLS / Moody's.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline for Hormuz — Trump threatens power plants + bridges — 82nd Airborne deploying — 3 CSGs committed — daily costs $890M-$2B
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 38: ~$1.0-1.5B/day (range $890M-$2B). Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline: 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day.' 82nd Airborne deploying 2,000-3,000 paratroopers. 6,000-8,000 ground troops positioning near Iran. Three CSGs committed: Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (Eastern Med), Bush (Atlantic transit ETA Apr 10-12). 45-day ceasefire rejected by Iran. Pentagon $200B+ supplemental faces massive bipartisan resistance. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / USNI / Reuters.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (38 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$45.6B through Day 38 — $200B supplemental faces massive bipartisan resistance — Progressive Caucus opposes — 250+ orgs signed letter against — Boebert/Clyde/Roy (R) balking — no vote scheduled — Trump approval 28% (war-specific)
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 38: ~$45.6B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental faces massive bipartisan resistance — Congressional Progressive Caucus opposes, 250+ organizations signed letter against, Boebert/Clyde/Roy (R) balking. No vote scheduled. War Powers resolutions failed (Senate Mar 5, House Mar 6). War-specific approval: 28% (YouGov). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / YouGov / Congress.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions — Adm. Cooper: 'no navy sailing, no aircraft flying, air defenses largely destroyed' — 155+ warships/subs destroyed (~92% of Iran's fleet) — first sub sinking since WWII (MK-48 torpedo)
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (as of Apr 1-2): 12,300+ military targets across 13,000+ missions. Adm. Brad Cooper: 'We don't see their navy sailing. We don't see their aircraft flying, and their air and missile defense systems have largely been destroyed.' Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,000+ through Day 38. 155+ warships and submarines destroyed (~92% of Iran's large warships). US fast-attack submarine sank Iranian submarine with MK-48 torpedo — first such engagement since WWII. Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers and 1,000+ ballistic missiles. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / CSIS.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 15 US KIA confirmed — 140-520+ WIA (Pentagon vs Intercept) — F-15E pilot shot down and rescued — WSO rescued after 48+ hrs inside Iran — 7 aircraft lost + 12+ MQ-9 drones
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 15 KIA confirmed as of Apr 1. Pentagon official figure ~140 wounded (Mar 10) disputed by The Intercept (520+ including smoke inhalation aboard USS Ford). F-15E WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading IRGC — CIA deception campaign + makeshift airbase inside Iran. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes (3 F-15Es Kuwait friendly fire Mar 2; 1 KC-135 Mar 12; 1 F-15E shot down Apr 3; 1 A-10 Apr 3) + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Key incidents: 6 KIA Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 KIA KC-135 crash Iraq (Mar 12). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 140-520+ WIA (Pentagon vs Intercept) — Pentagon official ~140 WIA (Mar 10) — The Intercept: 520+ — 'casualty cover-up' — SecDef Hegseth fired 12+ senior leaders
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. Pentagon official figure: ~140 WIA (Mar 10). The Intercept reports 520+ including smoke inhalation incidents aboard USS Ford, accusing Pentagon of 'casualty cover-up.' SecDef Hegseth fired Army Chief Gen. Randy George — 12+ senior military leaders purged since taking office. Five former defense secretaries condemned the pattern as 'reckless.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Reuters.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded — HRANA: 3,519+ (1,607 civilians, 244+ children) — Hengaw: 7,300+ (890 civilian, 6,410 military) — 3.2M displaced — ~20,000 civilian buildings affected
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and internet blackout. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded. HRANA: 3,519+ (1,607 civilians, 244+ children). Hengaw (Kurdish rights): 7,300+ (890 civilians, 6,410 military in 34 days). Iran International: 4,700+ security forces. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~8,500+. Iran retains ~50% of missile launchers and 1,000+ ballistic missiles. ~20,000 civilian buildings affected. 307 health/medical facilities damaged. 3.2 million displaced. IAEA severed since Feb 26. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / OCHA / IAEA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,200+ killed — 3,500+ wounded — ~1M displaced — IDF expanding ground operations toward Litani — permanent buffer zone south of Litani — Hezbollah 100s of rockets daily — 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed — UNSC voted to terminate UNIFIL by end 2026
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli ground invasion since March 2. 1,200+ killed. 3,500+ wounded. ~1 million displaced. Israel expanded ground operations, establishing permanent buffer zone south of Litani River. Hezbollah continues firing hundreds of rockets daily at Israel. Israel killed Hezbollah southern front commander Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem in Beirut strike Apr 1. 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers killed. Security Council voted to terminate UNIFIL mission by end 2026. Nearly one million Lebanese displaced. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / UNICEF / Al Jazeera / IDF / UNHCR.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.2M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M (incl. ~864,000 children) — additional 4M across region (Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan) — Lebanon 1.2M (1/6 of pop.) — 115M need humanitarian aid across Eastern Med
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR estimates 3.2 million internally displaced in Iran (including ~864,000 children). An additional 4 million displaced across the region (Lebanon, Iraq, Jordan). Total: 7.2M+. Lebanon: 1.2 million displaced (1/6 of population), with 189,000+ crossing into Syria and 370,000+ children affected. 115 million people across Eastern Mediterranean require humanitarian assistance. WHO $30.3M flash appeal (Mar-Aug 2026). 20+ verified attacks on Iranian healthcare (WHO). Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — 400+ vessels stranded Gulf of Oman — 53 transits last week (up from 36) — still 90%+ below normal — 20,000+ seafarers stranded — Apr 7 deadline looms
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. 400+ vessels anchored in Gulf of Oman. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. Lloyd's List: 53 transits last week, up from 36 prior week, but still a fraction of normal volume. CMA CGM container vessel and Japanese gas carrier — first Western-linked ships to transit since war began (Apr 4). Strait traffic down 90%+ from pre-war levels, removing ~12-15M bpd from global markets. Hull war risk premiums 1.5-3% of vessel value ($10-14M per VLCC). IRGC charging $1/barrel tolls in yuan/stablecoins. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / IEA.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 244+ children (Iran) + UNICEF: 121+ (Lebanon) + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Minab school (165-180 killed) — Pentagon: 'targeting error rooted in stale intelligence' — UN: potential war crime
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran as of April 4. UNICEF: 121+ children killed in Lebanon, 399 injured. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Deadliest single incident: Minab girls' school (Feb 28) — 165-180 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12. Pentagon acknowledged US was 'likely responsible' due to 'targeting error rooted in stale intelligence data.' UN characterized it as potential war crime. IRGC child soldier recruitment (children as young as 12) condemned by Amnesty International and HRW. 'Janfada' volunteer campaign claims 7M registrants. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / Amnesty International.
